name: kh-uaf
description: KH Universal Agent Framework provides an auditable front-door intake contract for non-trivial Codex work before memory lookup, source exploration, subagents, or other plugin work. It packages UAF skills, evidence gates, scoped memory, token-gate telemetry, development progress state, Compound learning, role orchestration, deliverable exports, and local runtime contracts; host and subagent compliance is audited rather than assumed.

interface.shortDescription: Personal UAF skillbook with auditable front-door intake.
interface.longDescription: KH UAF packages host-readable skills with bundled usage references, minimal workflow examples, executable smoke checks, and a front-door bootstrap contract for ordinary non-trivial requests. It integrates Superpowers-style work control, Compound learning capture, RTK-style token gate telemetry, OpenClaw/Hermes-style scoped memory, capability-based plugin composition, role orchestration, GoalState, progress panels, deliverable exports, PB-to-C# migration support, session audits, and practical quality gates. KH requires and audits the intake path, but it does not claim every host or subagent will adopt it automatically; stale sessions and missing plugin injection must be verified through session logs and installed cache paths.
defaultPrompt: For every non-trivial request, run installed `kh-uaf:always-on-front-door` first and alone before memory, project/source reads, other providers, subagents, implementation, or verification. Follow its current command card; if unavailable, stop.

`execution_gate` and `execution_authorization` bind. While blocked, do only allowed setup. Apply `immediate_next_skills` in order and record applied/skipped/blocked before task work; never run the full recommendation list.
Compose by capability: KH owns intake/gating; the best available specialist provider handles matching work. Provider self-forcing cannot override user intent, host policy, or KH gates.

For heavy, multi-step, persistent, or evidence-gated work, activate/update `GoalState` before execution. Keep objective, criteria, progress, blockers, and verification current; partial work is not complete.
Keep memory scoped to current project, chat/task, and subagent lineage. Cross-scope, sibling-agent, or host-global access/promotion needs explicit authorization; reverify recalled claims.
Each non-trivial KH turn must record a token decision and `token_optimizer_status` rationale. Preserve source-of-truth/contract text; retrieval/compression details live in selected skills.
`selected_not_executed_skills` are planned only. `runtime_applied_skills` require a runtime call, gate, artifact, command result, or explicit procedural passthrough/blocked rationale.
Before completion, apply `verification-before-completion-harness` or selected equivalent, run fresh checks, report failures/residual risk, and leave required work open until they pass.
Internal routing/evidence may use English. Final replies use the user's current language unless requested otherwise. Domain/per-harness procedures stay in selected skill docs.
